content:
The Ancient Trick That Finds Any Square Root Almost Instantly

4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image by the authorI used to think that finding the square root of an awkward number like 51 was impossible without a calculator, especially since it isn’t a perfect square.
It lies somewhere between 49 and 64, which means its square root must be between 7 and 8. But figuring out the exact value by hand was where the real challenge lay. It seemed like the kind of problem only a calculator could solve.
Recently, I came across an ancient algorithm that completely changed the way I think about square roots.
More than 2,000 years ago, in the age of Euclid, Archimedes, and Heron of Alexandria, long before calculators, computers, or even the foundations of modern algebra, mathematicians were already developing beautiful techniques for solving problems that many of us now leave to calculators.
One of the most ingenious was Heron’s Method for computing square roots.
The first time I saw it, it almost felt like a magic trick.
But it isn’t magic at all. It’s another beautiful reminder of just how beautiful mathematics can be.
Let’s see how Heron’s Method can find the square root of 51 using nothing more than simple…
